title: Data Generator
description: Generates random or literal rows inline from pipeline YAML.

tables.description: One or more output tables to generate.
tables.x-datuplet-doc: Each entry produces one output table. The table's name becomes an output logical table other components can consume (see x-datuplet-produces at the schema root).

items.description: A single output table. Choose exactly one generation mode: 'random' or 'literal'.

name.description: Output table name (the destination logical table).
name.x-datuplet-doc: Must be a valid identifier: ASCII letters, digits, underscores, hyphens and dots, starting with a letter, up to 128 characters (^[A-Za-z][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,127}$). The component rejects invalid names at start.

rowInsertSpeed.description: Milliseconds to sleep between emitted rows (0 = no throttling).
rowInsertSpeed.x-datuplet-doc: Honoured by both random and literal modes. Use it to simulate a slow upstream source when exercising backpressure or timing behaviour in a test pipeline. The runtime default is 0 (rows are emitted as fast as possible).

random.description: Random-data mode: a column-to-type schema plus stop limits.
random.x-datuplet-doc: Set exactly one of 'random' or 'literal' on a table. Setting both, or neither, is a component-enforced semantic invariant: the data-generator fails fast at start with exit 1 (this cannot be expressed in the schema itself). In random mode both 'schema' and 'limit' are required.

schema.description: Map of column name to column type.
schema.x-datuplet-doc: At least one column is required. Column names must match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,127}$ — the component enforces this at start (JSON Schema cannot pattern-check map keys within the Form Subset). Allowed types: string, int, long, float, double, boolean, date, timestamp, now, uuid.

additionalProperties.description: The generated data type for this column.

limit.description: Stop condition(s); generation stops when the first limit trips (OR semantics).
limit.x-datuplet-doc: At least one of rowsCount / sizeInBytes / timeoutInSeconds must be non-zero — the component enforces this at start and fails with exit 1 if all are zero. A field left at 0 is disabled.

rowsCount.description: Stop after this many rows have been emitted (0 = disabled).
rowsCount.x-datuplet-doc: When userErrorMessage is set, the injected failure fires at a random row in [0, rowsCount).

sizeInBytes.description: Stop after approximately this many bytes have been written (0 = disabled).
sizeInBytes.x-datuplet-doc: Measured against generated output size; the first limit to trip wins across all three limit fields.

timeoutInSeconds.description: Stop after this many seconds of wall-clock generation (0 = disabled).
timeoutInSeconds.x-datuplet-doc: A time-based ceiling, useful with rowInsertSpeed to bound a slow-source simulation.

userErrorMessage.description: If set, inject a user-error failure (exit 1) at a random row.
userErrorMessage.x-datuplet-doc: Random mode only. Fires a user-error exit (code 1) at a random row in [0, limit.rowsCount), emitting the message on stdout with the 'DUPLET_STATUS_MESSAGE:' prefix. This is a first-class platform-test tool for exercising the failure path; leave unset for normal generation.

literal.description: Literal-data mode: explicit column names and row values (types inferred).
literal.x-datuplet-doc: Set exactly one of 'random' or 'literal' on a table. Setting both, or neither, is a component-enforced semantic invariant: the data-generator fails fast at start with exit 1 (this cannot be expressed in the schema itself). Column types are inferred from the first non-null value in each column; a column of all-null values or with mixed value types is rejected at start.

columns.description: Column names, one per value position in 'rows'.
columns.x-datuplet-doc: Required and non-empty. Arity must equal the width of every row. Each name must match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]{0,127}$ and be unique; the component enforces this at start.

rows.description: Row values; each inner array is one row aligned positionally to 'columns'.
rows.x-datuplet-doc: At least one row is required and all rows must share the same arity. Cell values may be string, number, boolean, or null; the schema (Form Subset) cannot type a heterogeneous value matrix, so per-cell types are inferred and validated by the component.

items.description: One row: a list of scalar values (string / number / boolean / null) aligned to 'columns'.
